headline: The Hydration Hierarchy.
objective: Introduce the educational difference between surface wetness and real hydration before the product cards.
concept: A split-screen macro beauty image contrasting superficial wetness with true deep hydration.

visual_structure.left_side: close-up macro skin texture with visible water droplets beading on the surface
visual_structure.right_side: close-up macro skin that looks plump, smooth, luminous, and deeply hydrated with no visible droplets
visual_structure.framing: tight macro crop, no full face, no product packshot
visual_structure.focus: clear visual distinction between wet skin and hydrated skin

art_direction.lighting: soft diffused light with a clean lab-beauty feel

must_have: left side must clearly read as surface water
right side must clearly read as deep hydration without visible droplets
clean negative space around the subject
prompt: Luxury skincare editorial hero image showing the difference between surface wetness and real hydration. Split composition. Left side: close-up macro skin texture with visible water droplets beading on the surface, glossy and superficial. Right side: close-up macro skin that looks plump, smooth, deeply hydrated, luminous, and healthy with no visible droplets. Clean scientific beauty aesthetic, cool white and pale blue tones, soft diffused lighting, premium lab-inspired skincare campaign, minimal composition, elegant and modern.
negative_prompt: product packaging, bottle, jar, full face portrait, hands, flowers, leaves, cluttered background, cartoon, CGI, 3D render, text overlay, watermark, dramatic splash, exaggerated water motion, low detail, blurry skin texture
